All right, what is a quarterly review? Let's go. A quarterly review is a dedicated time to discuss each teammate's performance at the past quarter reiterating what you communicate on your one-on-ones and addressing areas for improvement. Okay? Number one thing I want you to know, this should never be new news. Every time that I have one of these, I've never had one where I felt like I was delivering like this thing that's been building up and I'm like, oh man, this quarterly review is gonna suck because like they just sucked, you know. If somebody sucks, they should know that on their one-on-ones. That should be communicated the day they start sucking. The next day, the day after, and there should be an ongoing plan. And if that is the case, then on the quarterly review, you'd be like, hey, as you know, we're working on these areas of improvement. But let's talk about what you do well as well, right? Okay, so there should never be any new news in a quarterly review. If the only time you're letting someone know they're doing something badly is on a quarterly review, you're not doing the rest of the cadence correctly. Okay? So an example of how we do this. I provide you guys with an example, which is a download in here, but I find that this works the best and it's essentially, you know, you fill out all the basic information, but then it's breaking down their 4R document or their job responsibilities into this box right here. So primary performance expectations, their responsibilities, their goals, right? Maybe their goal as a salesperson is X amount of sales per quarter. Maybe their goal is to always keep their pipeline clear, whatever it may be in their job description. That each bullet for their job description responsibility goes right here. Next to it, what you're going to do is you're going to fill out all their job responsibilities. And then next to it, you're going to send this first to them and they're going to self evaluate. Okay, this provides them with a place to reflect and oftentimes they will give themselves more criticism than you ever would. Okay. After you get it back from them, you are going to evaluate yourself and put that right here as well as a performance rating. So there's outstanding above expectations, meets expectations, below expectations and needs improvement and you'll pick one of those to go right here. Then your quarterly review is you reviewing this document with that person. They've already filled it out and looked at it. You've already filled it out and looked at it. That part's not going to take the time. You're just going to meet and then review everything together so you can outline like, okay, this quarter, what do you personally need to work on? Where did you personally win? What are you really good at? What are things that you need help with? Okay, that's what you want to do during a quarterly review. All right. This also is a good time for someone to argue for a promotion. This is a good time for you to give a raise, for you to tell them and talk to them about their future in the company. Okay, so you want to make sure you're doing those things regularly, but this is a good time to have a more extensive talk on it. All right.
